Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Continued To Sep-14-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patricia Smith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Smith stated that she filed a waiver of accounting at the advice of someone that was providing legal assistance, that she did a final account and paid the taxes but that she has no funds to pay someone to do a petition, and that she has gone to every legal service out there but that she has not found anyone that is willing to assist her. The Court explained the importance/need of having a peition for final distribution filed. The matter was continued to September 14, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the petition for final dsitribution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Rob Harris.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-21-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanne Mirante for Watson, Hoffe & Hass. Patricia Smith in Propria Persona.

Ms. Mirante stated that the property was sold, that the funds were placed into the Estate account which is now closed and that the funds were depleted. In addition, Ms. Mirante stated that she received incomplete bank statements, and partial information on the disbursements and the unexplained withdrawals. Ms. Miranate also requested the Court grant the order to be relieved and allow Ms. Smith to close the estate. Ms. Smith argued that she has complied, stated that she agreed to allow Ms. Mirante to be relieved as Counsel and that she has contacted someone else to assist her with closing the estate. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). Status of closing of the estate was set for March 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jun-27-2007 Continued To Sep-21-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jeanne Mirante appearing for petitioner Patricia Smith. MaryAnn Dresher appearing for Mr. Rhodes. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Special Administrator. Case is ready to be set for evidentiary hearing. Both counsel agreed it will take two to four hours. Matter is continued to 9/21 at 9am for evidentiary hearing. Trial briefs are due by 9/14. Parties have agreed to authorized the special administrator to continue the clean up of the property and to distribute the contents.

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar May-02-2007 Continued To Jun-27-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jeanne Mirante appearing for petitioner Patricia Smith. MaryAnn Dresher appearing for claimant Rochefuculd Rhodes. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for special administrator. Mr. Ferdon does not agree with the examiner's notes which appointment of permanent administrator is required. The special administrator has general powers. Ms. Mirante wants an evidentiary hearing. Hearing will take one half day. Court accepted Mr. Ferdon's argument. The Public Administrator can continue to do the job as the special administrator. Matter is continued to 6/27 for status.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Jeanne Mirante appearing by phone for Patricia Smith. Mr. Rhodes or his attorney Lane Parker are not in court. The Public Administrator will only able to collect and administer the estate assets. The parties will have to file their own petition to resolve their issues. Court is appointing the Public Administrator as the Special Administrator with general powers. Letters will expire on further order of the court. Mr. Ferdon will prepare an order for instructions on the personal property and real property. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-04-2006 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-08-2006 Continued To Dec-04-06 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanne Mirante (Counsel for Patricia Smith, Petitioner); Lane Parker (Counsel for Rochefuculd Rhodes, separate Petitioner); Patricia Smith (Petitioner); Rochefuculd Rhodes (separate Petitioner). Jonathan Ferdon, Counsel for the San Francisco Public Guardian, specially appeared at the request of the Court.

The Court noted at the outset that in the absence of agreement between the competing Petitioners, it was inclined to appoint the San Francisco Public Guardian as the Administrator in this matter. After discussion and presentation by counsel and Ms. Smith concerning the alleged familial relationship of Mr. Rhodes to the decedent (or want thereof), the Court appointed the San Francisco Public Guardian as Special Administrator and continued the hearing on the competing Petitions to a specially set date and time of December 4, 2006 at 11:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 before Judge Dearman.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Trudy Verzosa.
